verdicts.claim: CR4 genre-primary mechanic (primary_genre_for/STASIS_PRIMARY_GENRE) is a dead lookup never consumed by the resolver, yet marked status:WIRED (wrapper.py:317) and claimed 'behavioral, not nominal' (packet line 193).
verdicts.reason: Confirmed by grep (resolver.py has zero references to primary_genre_for/STASIS_PRIMARY_GENRE/genre) and by empirical probe (changing the live stasis with no armature moves the mean track only 0.04 FACT/CONSEQUENCE/QUALITY via the pre-existing Stage-1 tense channel; no +1D genre-primary bonus exists in code). mechanics_selftest only asserts symbol resolvability, so WIRED is hollow. This is a genuinely nominal grounding claimed as behavioral, which the HARD RULE forbids overruling. Remedy: either plumb the +1D primary-genre bonus into _advance (and add a seeded resolution-outcome test), or demote cr4_stasis_genre WIRED->PARTIAL mirroring the honest audience_resistance F10 treatment and soften the docstring/packet claims.

verdicts.claim: The 'CR4 BEHAVIORAL' kernel test asserts only a pure lookup inequality (_pgf(FACT) != _pgf(CONSEQUENCE)); nothing in the suite proves CR4 changes how a contest resolves.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed at _kernel_tests.py:1167-1169 â€” the test body is three primary_genre_for lookups, no Bout/resolve()/track comparison, in contrast to the armature test at 1302-1340 which runs 400 seeded bouts. Gate C requires 'behavioral, not nominal'; CR4 has no resolution-outcome assertion. Remedy: add a seeded resolution-outcome test once the +1D bonus is wired, and stop describing CR4 as behavioral on the packet checker board until then.

verdicts.claim: RATIFIED CR5 supersedes the Doubt Marker (RATIFIED_2026-06-01.md:15 'Supersedes/target: Doubt Marker/Suspicion Token'), but the draft reuses and PRESERVES it (as the -2 backfire magnitude and success-side outcome), the opposite of superseding, and never surfaces the RATIFIED-supersedes vs Gate-B-ED-1060-keeps-and-extends conflict.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: RATIFIED line 15 frames CR5 as superseding the Doubt Marker; params/contest.md:112 keeps the Doubt Marker live (-2 to opponent's next winning margin); Gate B ED-1060 (Terminal Doubt) kept-and-extended it as a design-table commitment; rhetoric.py has no 'supersede' acknowledgment. The chosen path (preserve, align to later Gate B) is defensible, but the reversal of a ratified supersession relationship is a canon-fidelity gap that must be surfaced, not buried. This is a genuine Jordan-only design call, routed to open_decisions_for_jordan; deferred to an ED stub for the Synthesist to record.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub (contest_rebuild, ED-1062 packet Â§6): reconcile RATIFIED-CR5-supersedes-Doubt-Marker vs Gate-B-ED-1060-keeps-and-extends; document which relationship holds before settling CR5 canon.

verdicts.claim: CR5's backfire trigger is documented as an exchange-outcome contingency ('did not win its exchange, loses or ties it') but implemented as a solo reception-band threshold (deg < 2 against base_ob) with no comparison to the opponent's move.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: resolver.py:346 deg = self._reception(side) is a per-side degree() against self.v.base_ob; resolver.py:362 passes won_exchange=(deg>=2); CLASH/REINFORCE/CROSS/TIE appear only in a resolver comment (line 356), never in the resolution path. rhetoric.py:190-192 and packet Â§2 describe it as 'did not win its exchange (loses or ties it)', a comparative framing the code does not compute. Remedy: reword the trigger docs to 'a failed RECEPTION (reception degree < 2)', which also matters for the packet Â§6.4 loses-only-vs-loses-or-ties fork.

verdicts.claim: The deliverable ships TWO parallel armature formulations: the subtractive resistance path (armature_resistance/eroded_resistance/resistance_delta, ARMATURE_MAX_DELTA=1.0) marked WIRED is never called by the resolver; the only live path is the multiplicative resonance_uplift (ARMATURE_RES_GAIN=0.35). This is the 'second resistance mechanism' the lens forbids.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed by grep: resolver.py:296 is the sole live armature consumer and calls self.armature.resonance_uplift (0.35 multiplicative on res); armature_resistance/eroded_resistance/resistance_delta appear only in armature.py, wrapper.py registry, __init__ exports, and isolated kernel unit tests â€” never in a resolve() path. Two derivations of 'the same' effect with different constants and shapes ship simultaneously. Remedy: pick one channel and route the resolver through it; delete or mark-dead the other and its constant.

verdicts.claim: armature_resistance is marked status:WIRED (wrapper.py:316) but is never invoked in resolution; mechanics_selftest only checks symbol resolvability, so a dead function passes the WIRED gate.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: armature_resistance is not called by resolver.py (grep); mechanics_selftest (wrapper.py:327-332) only asserts _resolve(fn) is not None, and its docstring claims it vouches for mechanics 'live in resolution.' Contrast audience_resistance (wrapper.py:300), honestly marked PARTIAL for the identical derived-but-not-plumbed condition. Remedy: wire it or downgrade to PARTIAL mirroring audience_resistance, and strengthen mechanics_selftest to detect resolution-invocation.

verdicts.claim: The documented reuse target does not exist: the scope/comments claim armature.py reuses an existing Resonant-Style dot-product in sim/personal/conviction.py aimed at the opponent, but conviction.py contains no dot-product and no such primitive exists anywhere in sim/; armature.py writes its dot-product from scratch.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed by reading conviction.py in full (only Scar accumulation and a resonant_active set flag; no dot-product) and by grep (no Resonant-Style dot-product primitive anywhere in sim/personal). armature.py imports only .contract and .dictionaries; the dot-product is fresh at armature.py:236. The reuse is doc-level only (npc_behavior_v30 Â§1.3/Â§6.3 is a design doc). This is a wrong provenance/reuse claim, which the HARD RULE forbids overruling. Remedy: correct the docstring/MECHANICS source to state the primitive is NEW code grounded in the npc_behavior_v30 DESIGN doc (no pre-existing sim implementation); the internal single-sourcing (style_axis_alignment) is fine.

verdicts.claim: The asymmetric-proceeding gate-off is justified as preventing double-counting of 'the existing opponent-aimed Resonant Style', but that opponent-aimed dot-product is not implemented in the contest kernel, so there is nothing to double-count today.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: grep 'Resonant|resonant|style' in resolver.py returns nothing; opponent interaction is the structural dictionaries.derive_interaction (CLASH/REINFORCE/CROSS from genre/orientation), not a Conviction-vector dot-product. The Â§6.3 rationale references a design doc, not live code. The gate is cheap and correct in principle; the stated rationale is false today. Remedy: reword to 'forward-compatible: prevents double-counting once/if the opponent-aimed dot-product is implemented in code (currently only in npc_behavior_v30 Â§6.3)'.

verdicts.claim: CR4's central Gate-C promise ('stasis actually changes how a contest resolves, not just a label') is not realized: the stasis->genre map and F5/F6 classifiers are pure oracle functions with no consumer in resolution, yet cr4_stasis_genre is marked WIRED. (Rhetoric-fidelity restatement of findings 1/2.)
verdicts.reason: Same root cause as verdicts 1 and 2, independently confirmed: no consumer in resolver/modes/wrapper; a logos_spammer x300 probe on FACT vs CONSEQUENCE moves the mean track only ~0.056 attributable entirely to the pre-existing tense_weight, with no genre-primacy bonus in code. Remedy: wire the +1D primary-genre bonus into _advance with a seeded test, or demote WIRED->PARTIAL and stop claiming behavioral realization.

verdicts.claim: The armature's only citation-backed magnitude (ARMATURE_MAX_DELTA=1.0, grounded as npc_behavior_v30 Â§6.3 '+1 bypasses 1 point of resistance') governs the DEAD subtractive path; the LIVE resonance_uplift path uses an uncited [SEED] magnitude (0.35) applied as a multiplicative post-roll gain, a different pipeline location AND shape than the +1D-to-pool the cited source specifies.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: resolver.py:296 is the sole live consumer (resonance_uplift, gain 0.35); the cited Â§6.3 primitive is a +1D dice-pool bonus capped at +1D (npc_behavior_v30), not a multiplicative res scalar. The cited magnitude (1.0) and cited shape (pool dice) attach to the path that does not run; the running path uses an uncited number in a different pipeline location. The agonist's own self-attack concedes ARMATURE_RES_GAIN=0.35 is the softest/uncalibrated point. The conviction-sensitivity proof (aligned 9.04 > misaligned > flat) still holds via resonance_uplift, so this is a re-grounding, not a rebuild. Remedy: route the armature through the +1D-to-pool primitive Â§6.3 actually specifies (capped at Â§6.5 +1D) and delete/mark-dead the unused path; or retain resonance_uplift but drop the Â§6.3 grounding claim from the dead ARMATURE_MAX_DELTA and flag 0.35 as the sole live [SEED].

verdicts.claim: The CR5 self-Face backfire penalizes moves that PARTIALLY SUCCEEDED: a deg==1 Obscuring move that landed and pushed the mover's own track is nonetheless stripped -2 Face as a 'failed obscuring move'; and the -2 is applied to Face (a 0-10 credibility stat) whereas the cited Doubt Marker -2 is a track-margin quantity.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed by seeded probe (suppression, 600 seeds, single _apply): every backfired move (10/10, 100%) had also advanced the mover's own track (a partial deg==1 that landed), because deg = self._reception(side) is a solo roll with no opponent term. A nigrahasthana/foul is a move that fails as an argumentative act, not one that helped you, so the cost is mis-attached to partial successes. Separately confirmed params/contest.md:112: the Doubt Marker -2 is 'to opponent's next winning margin' (a margin quantity), while the backfire strips -2 from Face via Standing.strip (a 0-10 credibility stat) â€” same number, different quantity than the precedent. Remedy: gate the backfire on a genuine foul (deg==0 / a fault set), or compute an actual head-to-head margin; reconcile docstrings; note the -2-to-Face vs -2-to-margin quantity mismatch (already a listed fork, but docstrings overstate the precedent's fit).

verdicts.claim: For the flat/zero-vector adjudicator (the default), Success (deg2) and Partial (deg1) Appraise return dominant_axis:None / register:None under generic signal-promising notes, while only Overwhelming (deg3) special-cases the flat judge with an honest 'no armature to read' note. The genuinely-style-agnostic consequence is surfaced only at Overwhelming.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed by probe on appraise_armature(ArmaturePosition.zero(), d): deg1 register=None + generic note, deg2 dominant_axis=None + 'enough to pick a plausibly-aligned style', deg3 correctly notes 'no armature to read (flat/degenerate adjudicator)'. Since no canonical ADJUDICATORS_TABLE member carries an armature_position, zero-vector is the default path. A flat judge has NO convictions (surfaceable), not UNKNOWN ones, so this is a legibility gap, not the legitimate-hidden tension. Remedy (polish): give the flat case an explicit 'no discernible lean' payload at Partial and Success too, mirroring the Overwhelming branch.

verdicts.claim: The 'deliberately misleading' FAILURE read (picks the (i+1)%4 axis after the dominant) is not misleading when the judge has co-dominant axes: for ArmaturePosition(evidence=0.5, consequence=0.5) it returns 'consequence', an equally-dominant (equally-well-aligned) axis, silently handing the player a correct-enough style pick.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed by probe: appraise_armature(ArmaturePosition(evidence=0.5, consequence=0.5), 0) returns read='consequence' with weight 0.5 == the dominant weight, so the 'wrong' axis is co-dominant, undermining the intent that Failure is a real cost. Only occurs on co-dominant judges; boundary decision unaffected. Remedy: pick a misleading axis whose weight is strictly less than the dominant weight, falling back to the (i+1) rule only when no strictly-lower axis exists.

open_decisions: STYLE_AXIS 4x4 numeric projection: the STRUCTURE (each style's primary axis: Precedent->Evidence, Vision->Consequence, Suppression->Authority, Insinuation->Insinuation) is grounded per row in npc_behavior_v30 Â§1.3, but the MAGNITUDES (STYLE_AXIS_PRIMARY=1.0, STYLE_AXIS_OFFAXIS=0.15, ARMATURE_MAX_DELTA=1.0, ARMATURE_RES_GAIN=0.35) are [SEED] and correctly flagged as a candidate, not fabricated. Needs PP-674 vetting + sim calibration. The 4th axis (Insinuation) is a genuinely-new mapping since Â§1.3's 4th row is Solidarity (Knot-gated opponent vulnerability) â€” Jordan must ratify the Insinuation-axis identification.

Appraise-reveal boundary for armature_position: implemented as PARTIAL (register -> dominant axis -> dominant axis + coarse band; exact vector never revealed), best-grounded in Greif self-enforcing/undermining. Full reveal (rejected as self-undermining) and no reveal (rejected as opacity) recorded. Also open: whether Overwhelming should reveal the SECOND axis (implemented dominant-axis-only). Jordan's call.

Epideictic loss: the 2-genre (Memory/Projection) compression preserves the present/epideictic REGISTER (Qualitative terrain carrying Stasis.TENSE=='present' + the ethos_present RhetoricalWeights column, live in _advance) but loses a present-tense STANCE with its own +1D primary-genre bonus (no praise/blame style-card). Best-grounded recommendation: ACCEPT the compression; alternative is restoring a third Epideictic genre (cost: a 5th/6th style-card, breaks the 2x2 GenreÃ—Orientation grid). Stance-loss, not just a rename â€” Jordan's call.

CR5 self-Face-backfire trigger + magnitude: (a) exact FAILURE definition â€” loses-only vs loses-or-ties; note the current implementation is neither a true exchange comparison but a SOLO reception threshold (deg<2), so this fork should be re-framed against the actual mechanic before deciding. (b) whether the backfire scales with the mover's standing (deeper Nyaya: a high-standing orator risks more by stooping to jalpa); implemented flat -2. (c) whether the -2 should strip Face (0-10 credibility) or, per the cited Doubt Marker precedent, a track-margin â€” the reuse currently borrows the magnitude but applies it to a different quantity.

CR5 vs the Doubt Marker's canonical relationship: RATIFIED CR5 (2026-06-01) frames CR5 as SUPERSEDING the Doubt Marker/Suspicion Token, but Gate B ED-1060 (Terminal Doubt) kept-and-extended it, and the Stage-3 draft silently preserves+reuses it (aligning to Gate B). Jordan must decide whether CR5 supersedes, or keeps-and-extends, the Doubt Marker â€” the draft's silent reversal of the ratified supersession relationship should be surfaced before CR5 settles.

verdicts.claim: CR4 +1D primary-genre 'match' is a tautology / stasis-terrain gate, not genre discrimination; docstring describes an unreachable FACT-vs-CONSEQUENCE mismatch (rhetoric.py:143-145).
verdicts.reason: Repro confirmed: Stasis.relevant forces move.ground==live_stasis (primitives.py:21) before resolver.py:368, so genre_of_ground(mv.ground) and primary_genre_for(self.live) always derive from the same ground and can never disagree. pgpb fires 1.0 on every relevant merits ground (fact/def/conseq/feas), 0.0 on present grounds. The 'same move rewards different dimensions on FACT vs CONSEQUENCE' path faults out as evasion at resolver.py:353. It is a terrain gate, not genre-vs-genre choice. Kernel test _kernel_tests.py:1181 exercises _pgpb(FACT,CONSEQUENCE)==0.0, a resolution-unreachable path.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: re-scope CR4 docstrings + params cross-ref to state the +1D is a stasis-terrain (merits/genre-bearing) gate, OR give Move an independent genre (new mechanic) so a Memory framing on a Projection-primary stasis genuinely misses â€” the latter is a Jordan design fork.

verdicts.claim: The 'flat scalar becomes the DEGENERATE (zero-vector) row of this projection' unification claim is false; armature is a +1D pool channel while the flat audience-resistance scalar is a separate unplumbed subtractive channel.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: resolver.py has zero resistance computations (only a comment at line 277); wrapper.py:295-301 marks audience_resistance PARTIAL, 'DERIVED but not yet plumbed into resolution... Venue.base_ob is never set from it. metadata-only.' A zero-vector armature yields pool_bonus 0 (no pool change) = 'no armature bonus', not 'the flat scalar'. The two live on different channels; the armature neither subsumes nor makes the scalar degenerate. The critique's own FG-2 (critique.md:63) specified converting alignment into a *resistance delta on the erosion machinery*, with the +1D as the degenerate row of THAT â€” the revision instead built a parallel pool channel and kept the scalar unplumbed. Claim survives verbatim at armature.py:21 and is repeated across resolver/wrapper/__init__.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: either drop the 'flat scalar = degenerate zero-vector row' framing and describe the armature honestly as the first live judge-directed +1D channel while resistance stays unplumbed, or actually unify (plumb the flat scalar and armature onto one channel) as the scope asked.

verdicts.claim: CR5 self-Face backfire is cited as âˆ’2 but Standing.strip(2.0) applies âˆ’1.6 (STRIP=0.8); cited value != applied value.
verdicts.reason: Repro confirmed: Standing(6.0).strip(2.0) -> 4.4, delta 1.6. rhetoric.py:266 comment and CR5_SELF_GATING['magnitude'] (line 327) both assert 'âˆ’2 stripped from own Face (0â€“10 Standing)', anchored to the Doubt Marker âˆ’2; resolver.py:390 calls c.face.strip(2.0). The realized Face reduction is 1.6, not 2. Anti-fabrication (CLAUDE.md Â§7): the ledger-cited number and the code behavior must match. No test guards the realized delta.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: strip by 2.0/Standing.STRIP (or adjust Standing.v by 2.0 directly) for a true âˆ’2, OR restate the cited magnitude as âˆ’1.6 and justify; add a test asserting realized Face delta == cited magnitude.

verdicts.claim: The CR5 deg==0 backfire is not a NyÄya nigrahasthÄna (a formal foul); it penalizes a clean, licit, relevant argument that merely rolled net<=0. The real nigrahasthÄna machinery (FaultState) exists and is unused.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: the deg==0 backfire path is reached only AFTER SelfGating.licit (resolver.py:348), valid ground (350), and Stasis.relevant (352) all pass â€” so deg==0 is a clean/licit/relevant move that rolled low, not a chala/jati/hetvabhasa/evasion foul. Research Â§5.1/Â§12.6 defines nigrahasthana as enumerated points of defeat (self-contradiction, evasion, silence, quibbles), and resolver.py:319-353 already implements exactly these as FaultState (pratijna-hani/apasiddhanta/arthantara/chala-jati) with DefeatCatalogue clinch. rhetoric.py:239-247 cites the defeat-machinery it does not realize; a low roll on a valid argument is not a foul in the cited sense.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: attach the backfire to the existing FaultState channel (an Obscuring move that incurs an actual fault is the nigrahasthana the source names), OR drop the Nyaya framing and cite it honestly as an eristic-EV penalty. The exact trigger/magnitude/standing-scaling is a Jordan design fork.

verdicts.claim: STYLE_AXIS cites 'npc_behavior_v30 Â§1.3 Resonant Style Taxonomy â€” VERBATIM row â†’ axis', but Â§1.3 is an empty stub and the mapping / 4th axis are fabricated; the fabrication is encoded in a passing test.
verdicts.reason: The cited Â§1.3 IS an empty stub (npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:18-19 is a one-line header; Â§6.3 at line 118 is likewise empty), so 'VERBATIM row â†’ axis' is a fabricated/misattributed citation, and _kernel_tests.py:1327 asserts that non-existent 'Â§1.3 provenance' as a green test â€” the exact 'names a source it does not realize' anti-pattern. BUT the antagonist overstated 'the corpus does not supply a Style->vulnerability map': complete_systems_reference.md:20 (Status: CANONICAL) DOES map Precedent->Evidence, Vision->Consequence, Suppression->Authority â€” so 3 of 4 axes are genuinely grounded, just at the wrong file; the real 4th vulnerability is Loyalty/Solidarity, and Insinuation->Insinuation is a genuine new invention (the code self-flags this). Downgraded from blocker to major: the structure is not wholesale fabricated, but the cited provenance is false, the magnitudes are [SEED], and the test locks in the false citation. Not overruled â€” the scope explicitly forbids blessing a fabricated STYLE_AXIS value or a source-it-does-not-realize citation.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: strike the 'Â§1.3 VERBATIM' citation; reground the 3 real axes on complete_systems_reference.md:20 (and reconcile its retired 7-Conviction/older style-names against current canon); flag the 4th axis (Insinuation vs the real Solidarity/Loyalty) and ALL magnitudes as the STYLE_AXIS design fork for Jordan; fix _kernel_tests.py:1327.

verdicts.claim: The armature +1D is cited as Â§6.3 '+1 die â€¦ bypasses 1 point of resistance (cited shape AND magnitude)', but +1D-Argue and bypasses-1-resistance are two separate effects and the bypass is Evidence-only on-win; the code adds only a uniform pool die.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed against contest_extensions.md:45-48 (marked Canonical): '+1D Argue' is shared across all four MS but 'bypasses 1 resistance' is Evidence-only on-win; Consequence/Sanction/Solidarity have different on-win effects. resolver.py:278 adds pool_bonus ONLY to the dice pool; res (line 299) and base_ob (280) are untouched. So the realized mechanic is the well-grounded '+1D Argue' pool die (shared with CR4), but the 'bypasses 1 point of resistance' phrasing mislabels it. Behavioral core (a +1D pool die) is grounded; this is a citation-accuracy defect, hence minor not major.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: drop the 'bypasses 1 point of resistance' phrasing from the +1D cite (the realized mechanic is the +1D-Argue pool die); if a resistance-bypass is genuinely wanted, implement it as an Evidence-aligned on-win effect matching the source's per-style asymmetry.

verdicts.claim: The reuse premise is overstated: neither sim/personal/conviction.py nor npc_behavior Â§6.3 contains a dot-product to reuse; the armature dot-product is a new invention.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: conviction.py is ScarRecord/ConvictionState accumulation with no vector alignment; npc_behavior Â§6.3/contest_extensions is a binary style-match table (+1D on confirmed match), not a weighted dot-product; armature.py:248 is the only dot-product in sim/. Per the scope's explicit clause, a reinvented dot-product mislabeled as reuse is not to be overruled. It generalizes the binary style-match into a graded projection and reuses the STYLES_TABLE vocabulary + Panel-averaging shape â€” those reuse claims hold, but 'same dot-product primitive Â§6.3 runs' and the conviction.py source claim are false.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: soften the reuse language â€” the armature GENERALIZES Â§6.3's binary style-match into a graded dot-product (a new primitive) and REUSES STYLES_TABLE + Panel-averaging; do not cite conviction.py as a dot-product source.

verdicts.claim: The asymmetric-proceeding gate (adjudicator==opponent -> armature off) is a manual ArmatureConfig flag that nothing derives from the proceeding; wrapper/modes never set it, and no Royal Audience resolution-level test exists.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: opponent_is_adjudicator appears only in armature.py (definition/param) and _kernel_tests.py:1369-1373 (passed by hand); no occurrence in wrapper.py or modes.py, and modes.PROCEEDINGS['royal_audience']/['church_tribunal'] carry no armature-gating field. The gate works when the flag is set, but the anti-double-count guarantee is not wired to the proceedings it protects, and the scope-requested Royal Audience verification is done only via a hand-set flag, not a built proceeding.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: derive opponent_is_adjudicator from the proceeding role structure (royal_audience 'crown objects', church_tribunal 'inquisitor proposes') inside build_contest/resolve_contest, and add a resolution-level Royal Audience test asserting armature bonus == 0.

verdicts.claim: CR5 'advances/plants as before (Doubt Marker, ED-1060)' overstates: no Doubt Marker is planted in the resolver; a landed Obscuring move only calls _advance.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: resolver.py:376-378 comment says 'advances/plants as before'; the only Face mutation in resolution is the mover's own c.face.strip on a deg==0 foul (line 390). No opp.face.strip and no Doubt-Marker object exist; ED-1060 (dictionaries.py DOUBT_MARKER scope_note) itself states the resolver does not yet consume orientation and no Doubt Marker is wired. Deferring the plant half is in-scope, but the comment misdescribes current behavior.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: reword resolver.py:376-378 and rhetoric.py:233 to say the landed Obscuring move advances the mover's track and the Doubt-Marker plant remains the design-table-only ED-1060 mechanic, not yet wired.

verdicts.claim: The Success/Overwhelming Appraise bands return an armature-axis name (dominant_axis 'evidence'), not the style-card key ('precedent') the walkthrough Â§2 Step 1 ladder and player UI use.
verdicts.reason: Repro confirmed: appraise_armature(pos,2) -> {'dominant_axis':'evidence'}; walkthrough Â§2 Step 1 says Success = 'They favor Precedent' (a full STYLE) and Â§5 speaks in style vocabulary ('favors precedent'). STYLE_AXIS is 1:1 (precedent<->evidence) so the info is recoverable â€” correctly NOT an opacity finding, but an un-surfaced axis->style translation diverges the reveal from the ladder it declares it fits. The _AXIS_REGISTER (appraise.py:130) also inherits the Finding-8 misattributed Â§1.3 citation. The PARTIAL-reveal DESIGN decision itself is sound and well-grounded (Greif/Padgett).
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: at Success/Overwhelming also surface the aligned style key (invert STYLE_AXIS), OR amend walkthrough Â§2 Step 1 to speak in axes; reground _AXIS_REGISTER once the STYLE_AXIS citation is fixed.

verdicts.claim: EPIDEICTIC_COMPRESSION conflates qualitative stasis (classically forensic) with the epideictic genre.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed against research Â§1.4 (qualitative = 'granted the act and its name, was it warranted' â€” a forensic/past-act evaluation) and Â§12.5 (epideictic = present-tense praise/blame; the honourable/shameful is the epideictic axis). rhetoric.py:180 claims qualitative 'IS the epideictic honourable/shameful axis' â€” a mis-grounding. Low impact: the epideictic-LOSS question is genuinely addressed and flagged for Jordan (EPIDEICTIC_COMPRESSION['decision_for_jordan']), not silently ignored. Only the classical attribution is soft.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: soften the EPIDEICTIC_COMPRESSION 'answer'/'source' text â€” present-tense/epideictic survives as (a) the RhetoricalWeights *_present ethos register and (b) the kernel's design choice to tense QUALITY/JURISDICTION present â€” rather than claiming classical qualitative stasis IS epideictic.

verdicts.claim: DEFINITION is mapped to Genre.MEMORY (past), contradicting the ratified CR4 'definitional = higher-order reframe via the authored Present-rendering'.
verdicts.reason: The ratified CR4 text (RATIFIED_2026-06-01.md:14) does say 'Present-rendering', and 'Present-rendering' appears nowhere else â€” genuinely ambiguous, and the source-research Â§1.4 does not settle definitional's tense. rhetoric.py:89-90 asserts DEFINITION->Memory/past without flagging the tension. This is a genuine ratified-text ambiguity, not a clear code defect, so it defers to a Jordan flag rather than an outright fix.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: flag DEFINITION's genre/tense as an OPEN DECISION for Jordan (ratified 'Present-rendering' is unresolved), or ground DEFINITION->Memory against a citable reading; do not assert it silently.

open_decisions: STYLE_AXIS 4x4 numeric projection: the whole table is Class-B PP-674 design content. The STRUCTURE for 3 of 4 styles (Precedent->Evidence, Vision->Consequence, Suppression->Authority) IS grounded at designs/architecture/complete_systems_reference.md:20 (CANONICAL) â€” but cited wrongly to the empty npc_behavior Â§1.3 stub. The 4th axis (Insinuation->Insinuation) is a genuine new invention: the real 4th Resonant-Style vulnerability is Loyalty/Solidarity (Any+Revealing, Knot-gated), not Insinuation. Both the 4th-axis identification AND the magnitudes (STYLE_AXIS_PRIMARY=1.0, OFFAXIS=0.15) are unbacked â€” approve, revise, or re-derive the projection table.

Appraise-reveal boundary for armature_position: the agonist chose PARTIAL reveal (register -> dominant axis -> dominant axis + coarse band; exact vector never revealed) and documented it well against Greif/Padgett. This IS a decided, documented boundary (not a missing decision), but the partial-vs-full-vs-none choice, and whether Overwhelming should reveal the 2nd axis, are Jordan's design-authority call.

Epideictic-genre compression (3->2, Memory/Projection): does the loss of a first-class present-tense praise/blame STANCE (basilikos logos / pure epideictic) need a restored third Epideictic genre with its own +1D bonus, or is 'present-tense = qualitative terrain + ethos_present register' an acceptable compression? Agonist recommends ACCEPT; flagged as a stance-loss, not just a rename.

CR5 self-Face backfire exact quantity and trigger: (a) should the Face cost equal the Doubt-Marker track-margin -2 or be rescaled to the 0-10 credibility stat (note: the code currently applies -1.6, not the cited -2 â€” fix required regardless); (b) should the backfire scale with the mover's standing (deeper Nyaya self-gating); (c) whether deg==0 (a clean argument that rolled low) is the right trigger at all, or whether the backfire should attach to the existing FaultState nigrahasthana channel (an actual foul) instead.

DEFINITION stasis tense/genre: ratified CR4 says 'definitional = ...via the authored Present-rendering' (ambiguous, term used nowhere else); the kernel maps DEFINITION->Memory/past. Confirm the intended tense.

QUALITY-as-universal-start-ground: all 8 canonical proceedings open on QUALITY (present-tense, no primary genre), so CR4's +1D is dormant on the default agon path until a player shifts to a merits stasis. Confirm contests are intended to open on 'was it justified?' terrain with no genre bonus until a shift.

verdicts.claim: Finding 1 (blocker): CR4 mis-implemented â€” STASIS_PRIMARY_GENRE[DEFINITION]=Genre.MEMORY gives definitional a Memory primary genre, contradicting RATIFIED CR4 ('definitional = higher-order reframe via the authored Present-rendering') and reconciliation_map Â§1.2 res(3) ('definitional/DEFINITION -> NOT Memory ... never collapsed to a genre'); behaviorally live via resolver.py:368.
verdicts.reason: Verified end-to-end: primary_genre_for(DEFINITION)=Memory and primary_genre_pool_bonus(DEFINITION,DEFINITION)=1.0, so a Memory-genre move on DEFINITION-live earns the CR4 +1D via resolver.py:368 â€” DEFINITION is treated as Memory terrain. RATIFIED_2026-06-01.md CR4 (L14) calls definitional a reframe 'via the authored Present-rendering' (present, NOT past/Memory), and reconciliation_map_raw.md Â§1.2 defect(a)+res(3) rule it must NOT be collapsed to a genre. Capturing the reframe role separately (STASIS_ROLE='higher_order') does not cure ALSO assigning a genre â€” the contract forbids the genre. Neither rhetoric.py nor the packet cites Â§1.2 or acknowledges the deviation. This is a silent contradiction of the design contract every later stage must cite (DECISIONS.md L3/L71). Remedy: set STASIS_PRIMARY_GENRE[DEFINITION]=None (only conjectural/FACT sets Memory), keep is_higher_order_reframe as the sole DEFINITION handling â€” OR flag it explicitly as an open decision citing Â§1.2, not ship the contradiction.

verdicts.claim: Finding 2 (major): CR4's stasis->genre derivation routes through Stasis.TENSE (genre_of_ground = _TENSE_TO_GENRE[Stasis.tense(ground)]), which reconciliation_map Â§1.2 explicitly forbids as the genre intermediary ('Do not use TENSE as the genre intermediary ... CR4 supersedes it; qualitative/QUALITY -> genre by stance, not auto-present').
verdicts.reason: Verified: rhetoric.py:49-61 defines _TENSE_TO_GENRE and genre_of_ground on Stasis.tense(); the module comment (line 52) admits it reuses primitives.Stasis.TENSE. reconciliation_map_raw.md Â§1.2 (line 43) rules the map must be built on the stasis GROUND keyed to CR4's four stases, NOT on the TENSE tag ('a groundup-internal rhetorical-weight tag with no canonical standing'). This is the architectural root of Finding 1 (DEFINITION past->Memory is a consequence of the TENSE routing) and is behaviorally material. Remedy: re-key STASIS_PRIMARY_GENRE and genre_of_ground on a ground->{genre,operator} table per Â§1.2 res(1)-(4); keep CONSEQUENCE/FEASIBILITY->Projection (contract-endorsed). Fix jointly with Finding 1.

verdicts.claim: Finding 3 (major): the epideictic 'acceptable compression' grounding rests on a conflation the research doc explicitly corrects â€” rhetoric.py:180-183 and GATE_C_packet.md:90 claim 'a qualitative was-it-justified contest IS the epideictic honourable/shameful axis, Â§1.4', but Â§1.2/Â§1.4/Â§0.4 distinguish qualitative (forensic evaluation stasis) from epideictic (a present-tense praise/blame genre), and Â§0.4 downgrades this exact conflation.
verdicts.reason: Verified in research/rhetoric_oratory_contest_research.md: Â§1.2 (line 82) epideictic = coronation/eulogy present-tense praise/blame GENRE; Â§1.4 (lines 94-95) qualitative = the forensic 'quale sit â€” was it justified?' evaluation stasis; Â§0.4 (line 55) 'the honourable/shameful is the epideictic axis, not the deliberative one ... Do not treat [the six-item list] as confirmed canon.' The code equates the forensic qualitative stasis with the epideictic genre to argue 'epideictic survives as qualitative terrain', so the 'accept the compression' recommendation rests on a premise the source corrects. The open-decision flag itself is correct and stays; only the grounding is defective. Remedy: re-word EPIDEICTIC_COMPRESSION (rhetoric.py + packet:90) so the epideictic-survives claim rests on the defensible anchor (RhetoricalWeights ethos_present + Â§2.2 basilikos logos) and DROPS the qualitative=epideictic equivalence.

verdicts.claim: Finding 5 (major): the armature's central reuse claim is false â€” there is NO pre-existing Resonant-Style dot-product primitive in the corpus, yet armature.py asserts it reuses one and 'invented no new dot-product', while inventing the only dot-product in sim/personal.
verdicts.reason: Verified: the only dot-product/vector-alignment in sim/personal is armature.py:278 (style_axis_alignment, self-invented). sim/personal/conviction.py â€” the cited reuse target â€” contains NO vector/dot-product/alignment; its only 'resonant' surface is resonant_active: set[str] (a set of Scarred-Conviction names). armature.py imports only .contract and .dictionaries, nothing from conviction.py. The canonical Resonant-Style mechanic (npc_behavior_v30 Â§6.3:698) is a CATEGORICAL boolean '+1D to Argue roll on confirmed targeting', not a continuous dot-product. armature.py:65 states 'No new dot-product invented from scratch' â€” false. What is genuinely reused is only the STYLES_TABLE keys and the +1D magnitude/cap. This is a mis-documented reuse that will mislead maintainers. Remedy: correct the docstring to state the continuous 4x4 dot-product is a NEW primitive; flag as a Jordan decision whether the categorical Resonant-Style +1D, if ever ported, should be the degenerate case of THIS dot-product so there is one style-bonus primitive not two.

verdicts.claim: Finding 7 (major): CR5's ratified first half 'Indirect (Obscuring) -> attacks opponent Face (ethos)' is NOT realized in the resolver, yet the cr5_self_gating MECHANICS row is WIRED with source text 'Indirect->Face-attack + self-Face backfire' and a green test asserts orientation_channel(OBSCURING)=='face_attack' as if it realizes CR5.
verdicts.reason: Verified: in resolver._apply, opp is bound (line 316) but used ONLY at line 348 for SelfGating.licit â€” never to attack opponent Face. A landed Obscuring move (deg>=1) falls through to self._advance(side,...) at line 373, advancing the MOVER's own track byte-identically to a Revealing move; only the deg==0 self-backfire distinguishes Obscuring. orientation_channel(OBSCURING)='face_attack' is a dead string nothing consumes. The wrapper cr5_self_gating row (wrapper.py:329) source text claims 'Indirect->Face-attack' and is marked WIRED; the resolution-invocation selftest only verifies the self-backfire fires. The opponent-Face-attack is LEGITIMATELY deferred by Stage-3 scope (the Gate-B Doubt Marker is retained on the win path per RATIFIED CR5-vs-ED-1060), so remedy (b) applies: downgrade the cr5_self_gating source text to 'self-Face backfire only; opponent-Face-attack DEFERRED', re-label the orientation_channel test as a design-table lookup (not a wired channel), and flag the unrealized half as an open item for Jordan â€” do not present CR5's orientation half as fully grounded.

verdicts.claim: Finding 8 (major): residual Â§6.1 citation inversion â€” the Style->vulnerability tables and '+1D' magnitude are cited to the EMPTY npc_behavior_v30.md Â§6.1 (Appraise Revelation) while the populated Â§6.3/Â§6.5 are called stubs, in armature.py section 4 (lines 239/246/252/256/259/273/284/304/325/330/361), wrapper.py:323 ('Â§6.1 table (lines 698-701)'), and GATE_C_packet.md:259/329/335.
verdicts.reason: Verified against npc_behavior_v30.md: Â§6.1 'Appraise Revelation' (line 679) is EMPTY; Â§6.3 'Targeting Effects' (line 694) is populated with '+1D to Argue roll ... bypasses 1 point of resistance' at line 698; Â§6.5 'Stacking Limits' (line 736) carries the +1D cap at line 738. The agonist correctly fixed the docstring HEADER and kernel tests to cite Â§1.3 head (lines 32-42) + Â§6.3:698/Â§6.5:738, and the Â§1.3 head table IS genuinely populated (verified lines 32-42, with the 4th type = Solidarity) so the header re-pointing and the 'infill Â§1.3 is a stub' claim are correct. BUT the fix is incomplete: armature.py section 4 still says 'Â§6.1 table', armature.py:246 still says 'off the empty Â§6.3/Â§6.5 stubs' (INVERTED â€” the empty section is Â§6.1), wrapper.py:323 says 'Â§6.1 table (lines 698-701)' (self-contradictory: 698-701 is Â§6.3), and packet:259/329/335 repeat 'off the empty Â§6.3/Â§6.5 stubs'. A reviewer navigating by Â§-label is sent to the empty section, and the mislabel is locked in a MECHANICS source string. The line numbers (698/738) are correct; only the Â§-labels in section 4 + wrapper + packet are inverted. Remedy: correct every 'Â§6.1' cite of the targeting/stacking tables to 'Â§6.3'/'Â§6.5' and delete the inverted 'empty Â§6.3/Â§6.5 stubs' phrasing (the empty section is Â§6.1).

verdicts.claim: Finding 4 (minor): CR5 keeps the Gate-B Doubt Marker on the Obscuring-WIN path while RATIFIED_2026-06-01 CR5 lists 'Doubt Marker / Suspicion Token' as what CR5 SUPERSEDES; the draft presents the two as fully compatible without flagging the divergence from CR5's literal text.
verdicts.reason: Verified: RATIFIED_2026-06-01.md line 15 CR5 Supersedes column = 'Doubt Marker / Suspicion Token'; DECISIONS.md CR5 reconciliation row drops that clause; CR5_SELF_GATING['scope'] (rhetoric.py:343-347) asserts the Doubt Marker is unchanged (ratified Gate B ED-1060) with no note that CR5's ratified text targeted it for supersession. The reading is defensible (Gate B mediated it, and re-litigating the Gate-B Doubt Marker is out of scope), but the unacknowledged divergence from CR5's literal 'supersede' text should be surfaced. Remedy: one sentence in CR5_SELF_GATING + the packet CR5 section noting the retained-marker-plus-backfire is a Gate-B-mediated reconciliation of CR5's supersede text, not a verbatim application (cite RATIFIED L15 + DECISIONS + ED-1060).

verdicts.claim: Finding 6 (minor): the plan's 'flat scalar becomes the degenerate/zero-vector case of the new mechanism' is misleadingly satisfied â€” the flat audience_resistance scalar was never a live resistance mechanism, and the armature is an additive +1D POOL bonus, not a resistance mechanism; the plan-directed per-exchange resistance-erosion channel does not exist in the resolver.
verdicts.reason: Verified: MECH['audience_resistance'] status is PARTIAL 'DERIVED but not yet plumbed' (wrapper.py:301); the resolver's only 'resistance' reference is a comment at line 277 â€” no flat scalar or ED-295 erosion is applied. The live armature adds a +1D pool die (resolver.py:278/368-370), a distinct additive channel that returns 0 when absent, NOT a generalization of the flat scalar. IMPORTANT: on the ONE-CHANNEL sub-lens the code is genuinely CLEAN â€” the armature +1D and CR4 +1D share the single _reception pool_bonus channel, and the dead subtractive resistance path + uncited multiplicative resonance_uplift were correctly deleted (I confirmed no second mechanism). So keep the code as-is; the defect is framing only. Remedy: state plainly that the flat resistance scalar is unwired/metadata-only and the armature is a NEW additive pool-bonus channel (deliberately not the resistance mechanism the plan named); note to Jordan that the plan-directed resistance-erosion channel remains unbuilt if still wanted.

verdicts.claim: Finding 9 (minor): the CR5 self-backfire is triggered by a low reception ROLL (deg==0), not by a nigrahasthana (a formal foul), so the Nyaya 'points of defeat' grounding is named but not faithfully realized â€” the engine already models fault states with correct Nyaya names, and the backfire ignores them, firing on a clean low roll.
verdicts.reason: Verified by seeded probe (500 licit Suppression 'advance' moves on FACT ground): 7/500 backfired purely from a deg==0 reception roll; 0/7 had ANY structural fault flag set (evasion/barred/contradicted). The engine's actual nigrahasthana machinery lives at resolver.py:319/326/329/341/349/353 (apratibha, apasiddhanta, chala/jati, arthantara) and is never consulted by the backfire (resolver.py:388 fires on landed==False regardless of fault). So a perfectly relevant, licit, on-ground argument that merely rolls deg==0 is penalized as a foul though no fault flag is set â€” the 'genuine FOUL/nigrahasthana' label overstates what deg==0 is in the system's own vocabulary. The agonist's revision (deg<2 -> deg==0) was a real improvement but the label still outruns the mechanism. Trigger-semantics is a design fork: gate the backfire on an actual modeled fault state (true nigrahasthana), OR re-ground it honestly on Vacaspati's 'obstruction bounded by your own standing' (a failed bet, not a formal foul). Flag the choice for Jordan.

verdicts.claim: Finding 10 (minor): appraise_armature is inert (no resolver/wrapper call sites; its degree arg comes from no roll), yet GATE_C_packet marks Lens 2 PASS and asserts the finding-4 'fires in the resolver' standard for Stage-3 WIRED mechanics without disclosing that the reveal â€” the surface the opacity mandate leans on â€” does not fire in any playthrough.
verdicts.reason: Verified: appraise_armature call sites are only appraise.py (def), _kernel_tests.py, and __init__.py (re-export); resolver.py/wrapper.py have no appraise/degree wiring; the degree arg is a bare int produced by no Appraise-roll primitive. The armature CONSEQUENCE is wired and moves the verdict (resolver.py:368-370), but the reveal that would give the player a signal is not â€” so a genuinely-hidden adjudicator conviction currently yields no earned signal. This is appropriately deferred to Stage 6 (the Gate-C criterion is only 'decided and documented', and no Appraise reveal is wired anywhere yet), so the defect is the non-disclosure/consistency gap, not the missing wiring. Remedy: one sentence in appraise.py header + packet Â§4 stating the reveal wiring to an Appraise-roll->degree step is deferred to Stage 6; qualify the Lens 2 PASS as 'boundary decided; reveal not yet player-triggerable (Stage 6)'.

verdicts.claim: Finding 11 (nit): the Partial band's register (Revealing vs Obscuring, from the dominant axis only) is oversold as a steering signal â€” because off-axis leakage is 0.15 and the 4 styles split {2 Revealing, 2 Obscuring}, a judge's second-best style can be on the other side of the register partition, so trusting the register can steer the player away from a strong option.
verdicts.reason: Verified by probe: ArmaturePosition(evidence=0.6, insinuation=0.55) -> Partial register hint 'Revealing'; style alignments precedent 0.682 (Revealing, best), insinuation 0.640 (Obscuring, actual second-best), vision 0.172, suppression 0.172. The 'Revealing' hint points at {precedent, vision} (one great, one dud) and away from insinuation (0.640). The register is TRUE about the dominant axis but its billing as a useful style-picker is oversold. Remedy: soften the docstring/packet wording to 'the coarsest true signal about the judge's DOMINANT axis's register (not a ranking of styles)'; no code change.

verdicts.claim: Finding 12 (nit): the Overwhelming-band coarse-strength thresholds _STRENGTH_LOW=0.34 / _STRENGTH_HIGH=0.67 (appraise.py:106-107) are inline-flagged [SEED] but are NOT surfaced in APPRAISE_REVEAL_BOUNDARY['open_fork_for_jordan'] or the packet's open-decisions list â€” a player-visible band label with uncalibrated cut-points should be in the Jordan-facing disclosure.
verdicts.reason: Verified: appraise.py:106-107 define the thresholds [SEED]; APPRAISE_REVEAL_BOUNDARY['open_fork_for_jordan'] (appraise.py:93-97) and GATE_C_packet Â§6 (lines 280-283) list only partial/full/none and the second-axis question â€” neither lists the 0.34/0.67 strength-band cut-points. Anti-fabrication is satisfied (inline [SEED]), but a player-visible band whose cut-points are uncalibrated guesses belongs in the open-decisions surface Jordan reads at the gate. Remedy: add the 0.34/0.67 cut-points to open_fork_for_jordan + the packet Â§6 list as a flagged [SEED] calibration item.

open_decisions: STYLE_AXIS 4x4 projection-cell magnitudes (STYLE_AXIS_PRIMARY=1.0, STYLE_AXIS_OFFAXIS=0.15) are [SEED]/candidate, not ledger-backed â€” the whole numeric table needs PP-674 vetting + sim calibration before it becomes canon. (The +1D bonus magnitude itself, ARMATURE_MAX_POOL_BONUS=1.0, IS cited to npc_behavior_v30.md:698/738 and is NOT open.)

The 4th armature axis: the code names it 'Insinuation' (Projection+Obscuring style), but canon's 4th Resonant-Style/pressure-point vulnerability type is Solidarity/Loyalty (Knot-gated, Revealing) per npc_behavior_v30.md Â§1.3 head (lines 32-42) + complete_systems_reference.md Â§1.2. Whether the 4th armature axis should be Insinuation (as coded, a genuine new invention) or the canonical Solidarity is Jordan's call â€” as is reconciling complete_systems_reference.md's older style names (Citation/Prospect) against current canon (Precedent/Vision).

The Appraise-reveal boundary for armature_position: implemented as PARTIAL (register -> dominant axis -> dominant axis + coarse band; exact vector never revealed), best-grounded per the Greif self-enforcing/undermining reading. Alternatives: FULL (rejected as self-undermining/capturable) and NONE (rejected as opacity). Also open: whether Overwhelming should reveal the SECOND axis too. The hidden residual is legitimate tension, not an opacity bug â€” confirmed distinguishable from a real opacity finding.

Epideictic compression: the 2-genre (Memory/Projection) compression preserves the present/epideictic REGISTER (ethos_present + qualitative terrain) but loses a present-tense STANCE with its own +1D primary-genre bonus (no 'praise' style-card; basilikos logos / coronation oration has no first-class genre). Accept the compression, or restore a third Epideictic genre (cost: a 5th/6th style-card, breaks the clean 2x2 grid)? Jordan's call. NOTE: the current 'accept' grounding must first be re-worded off the qualitative=epideictic conflation the research Â§0.4 corrects (Finding 3).

CR5 self-Face-backfire specifics: (a) exact Face-cost QUANTITY â€” the -2 is anchored to the Doubt Marker's track-margin -2 but applied as a FIXED -2 points to the 0-10 Face credibility stat (a different quantity); equal-to-the-track-2 vs re-scaled-to-the-credibility-stat is Jordan's call. (b) whether the backfire should SCALE with the mover's standing (deeper Nyaya: a high-standing orator risks more by stooping to jalpa) â€” implemented as flat -2, scaling variant is a candidate.

CR5 backfire TRIGGER SEMANTICS (from Finding 9): the backfire currently fires on a plain deg==0 reception roll, NOT on the engine's own modeled nigrahasthana fault states (which exist with correct Nyaya names but are never consulted). Decide: gate the backfire on an actual modeled fault (true nigrahasthana/formal foul), OR keep the deg==0 'failed obstruction that lands nowhere' trigger but re-ground its label on Vacaspati's 'obstruction bounded by your own standing' (a failed bet, not a formal foul) so the label matches the behavior.

CR5's opponent-Face-attack half (from Finding 7): CR5's ratified text says 'Indirect -> attacks opponent Face', but Stage-3 scope retained the Gate-B Doubt Marker on the Obscuring-WIN path and implemented only the failure-side self-backfire. Confirm that deferring/replacing the literal opponent-Face-attack with the retained Doubt Marker (plus the new self-backfire) is the intended reconciliation of CR5, or direct that the opponent-Face-strip be implemented.

verdicts.claim: Finding 1 (major): CR4 +1D primary-genre bonus does not realize the ratified 'orator's CHOSEN genre matches primary genre' mechanic â€” it compares genre_of_ground(move.ground) vs primary_genre_for(live), and because Stasis.relevant forces move.ground==live it is tautological (structurally always-on for FACT/CONSEQUENCE/FEASIBILITY terrain; mismatch branch dead), dropping the orator's Style-card choice from CR4.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED against the real files + text. params/contest.md:61 states the condition literally as 'Orator's chosen genre matches [GM-set] primary genre'; CR4 (RATIFIED_2026-06-01 L14) re-grounds 'primary genre' on stasis but preserves 'chosen genre matches'. Verified in Python: primary_genre_pool_bonus(g,g) is fully determined by the live ground (FACT/CONSEQUENCE/FEASIBILITY=1.0; DEFINITION/QUALITY/JURISDICTION=0.0), and the resolver calls primary_genre_pool_bonus(mv.ground, self.live) only after Stasis.relevant (primitives.py:21) requires mv.ground==self.live, so the cross-ground mismatch branch (f(FACT,CONSEQUENCE)=0.0) is dead in a live bout â€” it faults 'arthantara' before reaching _reception. armature.style_of(side) exposes the chosen Style but feeds only CR5/armature, never CR4. This is a genuinely nominal (tautological) grounding of CR4's strategic half â€” exactly the class the ruling instructions forbid overruling. The packet Â§5 Lens-6 'PASS â€¦ Not nominal' overclaims. Remedy is a real design choice: either wire the chosen Style's genre into the CR4 match, or downgrade the framing and flag as deferred (mirroring the honest CR5 opponent-Face-attack deferral). Agonist should implement one; the wire-vs-defer choice touches STYLE_AXIS/genre coupling and is itself partly a Jordan call.

verdicts.claim: Finding 2 (minor): the asymmetric-proceeding gate (opponent_is_adjudicator) is never wired to the canonical royal_audience/church_tribunal roles and is exercised only by a hand-set boolean in a test; build_contest/_resolve_agon never construct an ArmatureConfig, so the gate is unreachable through the production path and the scope's required concrete Royal-Audience gate test is unmet.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. grep shows opponent_is_adjudicator has no producer except _kernel_tests.py:1409 against a plain _Adj5(); modes.py:424/427 set roles='crown_objects'/'inquisitor_proposes' but nothing maps those roles to opponent_is_adjudicator=True; wrapper._resolve_agon builds Bout(...) with no armature=, and build_contest constructs no ArmatureConfig â€” so the gate cannot fire through resolve_contest. The gate LOGIC is correct; what is missing is (a) a concrete proceeding-routed gate test and (b) a documented proceeding->gate deferral so it is not silently bypassed when the armature is later wired into _resolve_agon. Remedy is additive test + a flag; no behavior regression.

verdicts.claim: Finding 3 (nit): the gate-off rationale says it prevents double-counting the EXISTING opponent-aimed Resonant Style, but that opponent-aimed +1D-on-confirmed-match mechanic is not implemented in the contest kernel, so within this kernel the gate is prophylactic, not preventing a live double-count.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. grep for resonant|vulnerab|targeting across resolver.py/primitives.py/dictionaries.py returns nothing; STYLES_TABLE carries only the Doubt-Marker Obscuring behavior. The gate is correct and should stay; only the rationale wording overstates current kernel state (it forestalls a double-count ONCE the npc_behavior opponent-aimed mechanic is ported, which it is not yet). Wording accuracy fix only.

verdicts.claim: Finding 4 (major): CR5's self-Face backfire cites 'obstruction is bounded by your own standing' (Nyaya nigrahasthana / Quintilian) in five places and the reconciliation map's ratified CR5 decision requires gating by SelfGating.licit, but cr5_self_backfire(style_key, landed) has no standing parameter and strips a flat -2 regardless of the mover's Face â€” the central self-gating invariant of the cited source is behaviorally absent.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. Verified signature is (style_key, landed) â€” no standing arg; the resolver strips a fixed -2 via strip_points regardless of mover Face. The docstrings/CR5_SELF_GATING assert 'bounded by your OWN standing' (research Â§5.1/Â§5.3/Â§9.1) as the realized grounding, and the reconciliation map's ratified CR5 decision names SelfGating.licit gating. Standing-dependence is relegated to open_forks_for_jordan (c), not realized, while the mechanic is presented as the realization of self-gating. This is the 'names a source it does not realize' anti-pattern (Lens 6) â€” not overrulable per the ruling instructions. Remedy: either make the backfire standing-dependent (gate through SelfGating.licit and/or scale/cap the -2 by Face), or stop claiming it realizes 'bounded by your own standing' and DEFER that invariant honestly (mirroring the already-honest opponent-Face-attack deferral). The exact standing-scaling/cap is a Jordan design call.

verdicts.claim: Finding 5 (major): the off-axis STYLE_AXIS_OFFAXIS=0.15 partial-overlap weight â€” the thing distinguishing the 'continuous 4x4 dot-product' from a categorical +1D and the basis of the docstring's 'flat<misaligned<aligned' ordering â€” is behaviorally inert; roll_net does max(1,int(round(pool))), so any alignment below 0.5 rounds to zero extra dice and produces resolver outcomes byte-identical to the flat scalar. The wired armature is categorical (a 0.5-threshold step), not continuous.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED with live resolver evidence. sigma_leverage.roll_net floors pool via max(1,int(round(pool))); style_axis_pool_bonus('precedent', consequence=1.0)=0.15 -> round(13+0.15)=13 -> 0 extra dice. Ran the resolver over 800 seeds (TallyAtClose, FACT venue): FLAT and MISALIGNED band distributions are byte-identical {b:401,a:399}; only ALIGNED differs {a:429}. Reproduced the kernel-test scenario: flat==misaligned exactly (5.450456), aligned=5.627656. The kernel test at line 1457 asserts _flat <= _misaligned (<=, not <) â€” the <= is load-bearing precisely because they are equal â€” while its human-readable description string claims 'flat < misaligned < aligned', which is false. The armature.py docstring's 'continuous dot-product / off-axis partial-overlap / flat<misaligned<aligned' is not the realized behavior. Remedy: either make the fractional bonus behaviorally live (round once at the summed-bonus point, or route sub-die alignment through the net_boost/mu-shift channel), or correct the docstring + test description to state the wired bonus is a 0.5-threshold categorical +1D and re-examine whether a NEW continuous primitive is justified when the wired result is categorical. Not a no-repro; not overrulable.

verdicts.claim: Finding 6 (minor): CR4's Memory (FACT) primary-genre branch is unreachable in canonical play â€” all eight proceedings start on QUALITY (primary_genre None), and shift only moves UP the Stasis ladder, so FACT (below QUALITY) can never be reached; only Projection (CONSEQUENCE/FEASIBILITY) can fire CR4 on a shipped venue. Every CR4 behavioral test uses a synthetic FACT-start venue no proceeding uses; the forensic-past branch is inert on the shipped surface and this is not flagged.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. Verified all 8 modes.proceeding_venue(pn).start_ground == 'quality' with primary_genre_for('quality') is None; Stasis.stronger_than('fact','quality')=False, stronger_than('consequence','quality')=True. So FACT/DEFINITION (ladder indices 0,1) are unreachable via up-only shift from QUALITY (index 2); only CONSEQUENCE/FEASIBILITY are reachable. grep confirms rhetoric.py flags no Memory-branch reachability gap. This is a genuine reachability/coverage gap: half the CR4 mechanic is inert in canonical play, and whether QUALITY-default proceedings deliberately foreclose Memory-primary is an open question. Remedy: add a CR4 behavioral test that fires through a real proceeding (shift to CONSEQUENCE in grand_contest) and document the Memory-branch reachability constraint; flag the intended-vs-gap question for Jordan.

verdicts.claim: Finding 7 (nit): the armature applies a uniform +1D for every style, but its cited source npc_behavior_v30 Â§6.3 gives each Resonant Style a DISTINCT on-confirm effect beyond the shared +1D (Evidence bypasses resistance, Consequence +2 strain, Authority Doubt Marker, Solidarity Ob-1); the armature docstring quotes the Evidence row's '+1D â€¦ bypasses 1 point of resistance' as representative of a uniform mechanic when the source is explicitly heterogeneous.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. npc_behavior_v30.md Â§6.3 Targeting Effects table shows four distinct on-confirm effects; the armature's STYLE_AXIS gives every style the same _row shape -> uniform +1D magnitude. Defensible simplification because the armature is judge-aimed and the strain/Doubt/Belief effects are opponent-side, and it is honestly [SEED]-flagged â€” hence a nit. Remedy: note in the docstring that Â§6.3's per-style effects are opponent-directed and deliberately not carried into the judge-aimed armature (citation is to the +1D magnitude common to all rows, not the Evidence row's specific resistance-bypass). Whether the judge-aimed armature should ever differentiate by style is a minor Jordan question.

verdicts.claim: Finding 8 (minor): appraise.py violates its own 'consequence class surfaced at every band / never null' invariant for a flat (zero-vector) adjudicator â€” the Partial and Success bands return a bare None with a note describing signal that does not exist, so the resolved consequence (flat judge -> armature channel dead -> all styles equal) is not surfaced at 2 of 4 bands; only Overwhelming handles the flat case honestly.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. Ran appraise_armature(ArmaturePosition.zero(), d): Partial -> {'register':None, note:'the judge's register â€¦ coarsest true signal'}; Success -> {'dominant_axis':None, note:'the judge's dominant armature axis â€” enough to pick a plausibly-aligned style'} (asserts an axis 'enough to pick a style' while handing back None). Only Overwhelming returns the honest 'no armature to read (flat/degenerate adjudicator)'. Non-flat judges reveal correctly. This contradicts appraise.py:84-85 and packet Â§4's 'consequence class surfaced at every band, never null'. Remedy: return an explicit flat-adjudicator signal (register/dominant_axis None WITH a 'no exploitable lean â€” flat/degenerate; armature channel inactive' note) at Partial/Success, matching Overwhelming, plus a kernel guard. This is the case the player most needs told plainly, so it is a legibility (opacity) finding, not the legitimate-hidden-conviction case.

verdicts.claim: Finding 9 (nit): the Gate C packet Â§4 (Appraise-reveal section) cites the EMPTY npc_behavior_v30 Â§6.1 as 'the same shape used to read the opponent â€¦ pointed at the judge' â€” the exact Â§6.1 mislabel finding 8 elsewhere corrected; Â§6.1 'Appraise Revelation' is blank and the real reused Appraise-step prose is at :387, which appraise.py itself cites correctly.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. GATE_C_packet.md Â§4 reads 'the same shape used to read the opponent, npc_behavior_v30 Â§6.1, pointed at the judge'; npc_behavior_v30.md Â§6.1 'Appraise Revelation' (line ~679) is empty; the live Contest Appraise step is at :387, which appraise.py:52-57 cites correctly and the packet's own Lens-5 row names Â§6.1 as empty â€” so Â§4 was not updated to match. Remedy: replace the Â§6.1 citation in packet Â§4 with npc_behavior_v30.md:387 (matching appraise.py), so no packet section cites the empty Â§6.1 as a provenance source.

open_decisions: STYLE_AXIS 4x4 numeric projection cells (STYLE_AXIS_PRIMARY=1.0, STYLE_AXIS_OFFAXIS=0.15): Class-B, PP-674-vetted, needs citation. The +1D magnitude itself is cited (npc_behavior_v30.md:698/:738); the primary=1.0 and off-axis=0.15 cell weights are [SEED] and the whole table is a flagged candidate. NOTE (finding 5): as wired, the 0.15 off-axis weight is behaviorally inert (rounds to 0 dice), so whichever way this is decided, Jordan should also decide whether the armature is intended to be continuous (requiring the sub-die bonus to be made live via a mu-shift/single-round change) or categorical (a 0.5-threshold +1D, in which case the off-axis cell is documentation-only).

The 4th armature axis: Insinuation (as coded) vs the canonical 4th Resonant-Style/pressure-point type Solidarity/Loyalty (Knot-gated, Revealing). npc_behavior_v30 Â§1.3 / complete_systems_reference Â§1.2 make the 4th type Solidarity; the code's Insinuation (Projection+Obscuring) is a new invention. Also reconcile the older style names (Citation/Prospect) in complete_systems_reference vs the code's Precedent/Vision.

The Appraise-reveal boundary for armature_position: full / partial / no reveal. The agonist implemented PARTIAL on the existing 4-band ladder (best-grounded via Greif self-enforcing/undermining + Padgett robust action). Also open per appraise.py: whether Overwhelming should reveal the SECOND axis too.

Epideictic compression (Aristotle's third genre): accept the 2-genre (Memory/Projection) compression with epideictic surviving only as the RhetoricalWeights ethos_present register, OR restore a third Epideictic genre with its own primary-genre bonus (cost: a 5th/6th style card, breaks the clean 2x2 grid). Agonist recommends ACCEPT; flagged as a stance-loss, not just a rename.

CR5 self-Face backfire specifics: (a) whether/when to wire CR5's DEFERRED opponent-Face-attack half (and retire the Gate-B Doubt Marker) â€” currently a landed Obscuring move advances the mover's own track rather than stripping the opponent's Face; (b) whether the self-Face cost should equal the Doubt Marker's track-margin -2 or be re-scaled to the 0-10 Face credibility stat; (c) whether the backfire should be standing-dependent / scale with the mover's Face (deeper Nyaya self-gating: a high-standing orator risks more by stooping to jalpa) â€” currently a flat -2 with no standing parameter (see finding 4).

CR4 chosen-genre wiring (finding 1): whether the CR4 +1D should be re-wired to compare the orator's chosen Style genre (Precedent=Memory / Vision=Projection) against the live-stasis primary genre â€” restoring the ratified 'chosen genre matches' strategic decision â€” or whether collapsing genre to terrain is an accepted Stage-3 simplification to be flagged/deferred honestly.

CR4 Memory-branch reachability (finding 6): whether QUALITY-default proceedings deliberately foreclosing the Memory/forensic-past primary genre (FACT unreachable via up-only shift) is intended, or a reachability gap that should be addressed by authoring a below-QUALITY start_ground or a down-shift allowance for some proceeding.
